acts_as_attachable :after_add => :attachment_added, :after_remove => :attachment_removed
  acts_as_customizable
  acts_as_watchable
  acts_as_searchable :columns => ['subject', "#{table_name}.description"],
                     :preload => [:project, :status, :tracker],
                     :scope => lambda {|options| options[:open_issues] ? self.open : self.all}

  acts_as_event :title => Proc.new {|o| "#{o.tracker.name} ##{o.id} (#{o.status}): #{o.subject}"},
                :url => Proc.new {|o| {:controller => 'issues', :action => 'show', :id => o.id}},
                :type => Proc.new {|o| 'issue' + (o.closed? ? '-closed' : '')}

  acts_as_activity_provider :scope => proc {preload(:project, :author, :tracker, :status)},
                            :author_key => :author_id

  acts_as_mentionable :attributes => ['description']
  acts_as_webhookable
  include Issue::Webhookable

  DONE_RATIO_OPTIONS = %w(issue_field issue_status)

  attr_reader :transition_warning
  attr_writer :deleted_attachment_ids
  delegate :notes, :notes=, :private_notes, :private_notes=, :to => :current_journal, :allow_nil => true

  validates_presence_of :subject, :project, :tracker
  validates_presence_of :priority, :if => Proc.new {|issue| issue.new_record? || issue.priority_id_changed?}
  validates_presence_of :status, :if => Proc.new {|issue| issue.new_record? || issue.status_id_changed?}
  validates_presence_of :author, :if => Proc.new {|issue| issue.new_record? || issue.author_id_changed?}

  validates_length_of :subject, :maximum => 255
  validates_inclusion_of :done_ratio, :in => 0..100
  validates :estimated_hours, :numericality => {:greater_than_or_equal_to => 0, :allow_nil => true, :message => :invalid}
  validates :start_date, :date => true
  validates :due_date, :date => true
  validate :validate_issue, :validate_required_fields, :validate_permissions

  scope :visible, (lambda do |*args|
    joins(:project).
    where(Issue.visible_condition(args.shift || User.current, *args))
  end)

  scope :open, (lambda do |*args|
    is_closed = !args.empty? ? !args.first : false
    joins(:status).
    where(:issue_statuses => {:is_closed => is_closed})
  end)

  scope :recently_updated, lambda {order(:updated_on => :desc)}
  scope :on_active_project, (lambda do
    joins(:project).
    where(:projects => {:status => Project::STATUS_ACTIVE})
  end)
  scope :fixed_version, (lambda do |versions|
    ids = [versions].flatten.compact.map {|v| v.is_a?(Version) ? v.id : v}
    ids.any? ? where(:fixed_version_id => ids) : none
  end)
  scope :assigned_to, (lambda do |arg|
    arg = Array(arg).uniq
    ids = arg.map {|p| p.is_a?(Principal) ? p.id : p}
    ids += arg.grep(User).map(&:group_ids).flatten.uniq
    ids.compact!
    ids.any? ? where(:assigned_to_id => ids) : none
  end)
  scope :like, (lambda do |q|
    if q.present?
      where(*::Query.tokenized_like_conditions("#{table_name}.subject", q))
    end
  end)

  before_save :close_duplicates, :update_done_ratio_from_issue_status,
              :force_updated_on_change, :update_closed_on
  after_save do |issue|
    if !issue.saved_change_to_id? && issue.saved_change_to_project_id?
      issue.send :after_project_change
    end
  end
  after_destroy :update_parent_attributes
  after_save :reschedule_following_issues, :update_nested_set_attributes,
             :update_parent_attributes, :delete_selected_attachments, :create_journal
  # Should be after_create but would be called before previous after_save callbacks
  after_save :after_create_from_copy
  # add_auto_watcher needs to run before sending notifications, thus it needs
  # to be added after send_notification (after_ callbacks are run in inverse order)
  # https://api.rubyonrails.org/v5.2.3/classes/ActiveSupport/Callbacks/ClassMethods.html#method-i-set_callback
  after_create_commit :send_notification
  after_create_commit :add_auto_watcher
  after_commit :create_parent_issue_journal

  # Returns a SQL conditions string used to find all issues visible by the specified user
  def self.visible_condition(user, options={})

def create_journal
    if current_journal
      current_journal.save
    end
  end

  def create_parent_issue_journal
    return if persisted? && !saved_change_to_parent_id?
    return if destroyed? && @without_nested_set_update

    child_id = self.id
    old_parent_id, new_parent_id =
      if persisted?
        [parent_id_before_last_save, parent_id]
      elsif destroyed?
        [parent_id, nil]
      else
        [nil, parent_id]
      end

    if old_parent_id.present?
      Issue.transaction do
        if old_parent_issue = Issue.visible.lock.find_by_id(old_parent_id)
          old_parent_issue.init_journal(User.current)
          old_parent_issue.current_journal.__send__(:add_attribute_detail, 'child_id', child_id, nil)
          old_parent_issue.save
        end
      end
    end

    if new_parent_id.present?
      Issue.transaction do
        if new_parent_issue = Issue.visible.lock.find_by_id(new_parent_id)
          new_parent_issue.init_journal(User.current)
          new_parent_issue.current_journal.__send__(:add_attribute_detail, 'child_id', nil, child_id)
          new_parent_issue.save
        end
      end
    end
  end

  def add_auto_watcher
    if author&.active? &&
        author.allowed_to?(:add_issue_watchers, project) &&
        author.pref.auto_watch_on?('issue_created') &&
        self.watcher_user_ids.exclude?(author.id)
      self.set_watcher(author, true)
    end
  end

  def send_notification
    if notify? && Setting.notified_events.include?('issue_added')
      Mailer.deliver_issue_add(self)
    end
  end

  def clear_disabled_fields
    if tracker
      tracker.disabled_core_fields.each do |attribute|
        send :"#{attribute}=", nil
      end
      self.priority_id ||= IssuePriority.default&.id || IssuePriority.active.first&.id
      self.done_ratio ||= 0
    end
  end

  # Forcefully set the default value to any custom field values which are not
  # editable by the current user when creating a new issue. This may overwrite
  # existing custom values of a copied issue which are not editable by the
  # current user.
  def force_default_value_on_noneditable_custom_fields
    return unless custom_field_values_changed?

    editable_custom_field_ids = editable_custom_fields(author).map(&:id)
    custom_field_values.each do |field_value|
      unless editable_custom_field_ids.include?(field_value.custom_field_id)
        field_value.value = field_value.custom_field.default_value
      end
    end
  end

  def filter_projects_scope(scope=nil)
    case scope
    when 'system'
      Project
    when 'tree'
      project.root.self_and_descendants
    when 'hierarchy'
      project.hierarchy
    when 'descendants'
      project.self_and_descendants
    when ''
      Project.where(:id => project.id)
    else
      Project
    end
  end

  def roles_for_workflow(user)
    roles = user.admin ? Role.all.to_a : user.roles_for_project(project)
    roles.select(&:consider_workflow?)
  end
end

# Returns a SQL conditions string used to find all issues visible by the specified user
  def self.visible_condition(user, options={})
    Project.allowed_to_condition(user, :view_issues, options) do |role, user|
      sql =
        if user.id && user.logged?
          case role.issues_visibility
          when 'all'
            '1=1'
          when 'default'
            user_ids = [user.id] + user.groups.pluck(:id).compact
            "(#{table_name}.is_private = #{connection.quoted_false} " \
              "OR #{table_name}.author_id = #{user.id} " \
              "OR #{table_name}.assigned_to_id IN (#{user_ids.join(',')}))"
          when 'own'
            user_ids = [user.id] + user.groups.pluck(:id).compact
            "(#{table_name}.author_id = #{user.id} OR " \
              "#{table_name}.assigned_to_id IN (#{user_ids.join(',')}))"
          else
            '1=0'
          end
        else
          "(#{table_name}.is_private = #{connection.quoted_false})"
        end
      unless role.permissions_all_trackers?(:view_issues)
        tracker_ids = role.permissions_tracker_ids(:view_issues)
        if tracker_ids.any?
          sql = "(#{sql} AND #{table_name}.tracker_id IN (#{tracker_ids.join(',')}))"
        else
          sql = '1=0'
        end
      end
      sql
    end
  end

  # Returns true if usr or current user is allowed to view the issue
  def visible?(usr=nil)
    (usr || User.current).allowed_to?(:view_issues, self.project) do |role, user|
      visible =
        if user.logged?
          case role.issues_visibility
          when 'all'
            true
          when 'default'
            !self.is_private? || (self.author == user || user.is_or_belongs_to?(assigned_to))
          when 'own'
            self.author == user || user.is_or_belongs_to?(assigned_to)
          else
            false
          end
        else
          !self.is_private?
        end
      unless role.permissions_all_trackers?(:view_issues)
        visible &&= role.permissions_tracker_ids?(:view_issues, tracker_id)
      end
      visible
    end
  end

  # Returns true if user or current user is allowed to log time on the issue
  def time_loggable?(user=User.current)
    user.allowed_to?(:log_time, project) && (Setting.timelog_accept_closed_issues? || !closed?)
  end

  # Returns true if user or current user is allowed to edit or add notes to the issue
  def editable?(user=User.current)
    attributes_editable?(user) || notes_addable?(user)
  end

  # Returns true if user or current user is allowed to edit the issue
  def attributes_editable?(user=User.current)
    user_tracker_permission?(user, :edit_issues) || (
      user_tracker_permission?(user, :edit_own_issues) && author == user
    )

def destroy
    raise Unauthorized unless @issues.all?(&:deletable?)

    # all issues and their descendants are about to be deleted
    issues_and_descendants_ids = Issue.self_and_descendants(@issues).pluck(:id)
    time_entries = TimeEntry.where(:issue_id => issues_and_descendants_ids)
    @hours = time_entries.sum(:hours).to_f

    if @hours > 0
      case params[:todo]
      when 'destroy'
        # nothing to do
      when 'nullify'
        if Setting.timelog_required_fields.include?('issue_id')
          flash.now[:error] = l(:field_issue) + " " + ::I18n.t('activerecord.errors.messages.blank')
          return
        else
          time_entries.update_all(:issue_id => nil)
        end
      when 'reassign'
        reassign_to = @project && @project.issues.find_by_id(params[:reassign_to_id])
        if reassign_to.nil?
          flash.now[:error] = l(:error_issue_not_found_in_project)
          return
        elsif issues_and_descendants_ids.include?(reassign_to.id)
          flash.now[:error] = l(:error_cannot_reassign_time_entries_to_an_issue_about_to_be_deleted)
          return
        else
          time_entries.update_all(:issue_id => reassign_to.id, :project_id => reassign_to.project_id)
        end
      else
        # display the destroy form if it's a user request
        return unless api_request?
      end
    end
    @issues.each do |issue|
      begin
        issue.reload.destroy
      rescue ::ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound # raised by #reload if issue no longer exists
        # nothing to do, issue was already deleted (eg. by a parent)
      end
    end
    respond_to do |format|
      format.html do
        flash[:notice] = l(:notice_successful_delete)
        redirect_back_or_default _project_issues_path(@project)
      end
      format.api  {render_api_ok}
    end
  end

I now have a complete picture of the Issue contract and its dependents. Here is the full audit.

---

# Issue Contract Audit — Redmine

## Step 1 — Orientation

**Project:** Redmine, a Ruby on Rails 8.1 project-management / issue-tracking application (`Gemfile:5`). Standard Rails MVC layout under `app/` with substantial domain logic in `lib/redmine/` and a set of in-tree "acts_as" plugins under `lib/plugins/`.

**Layout:**
- `app/models/` — ActiveRecord domain (62 models). `app/controllers/` (~58), `app/helpers/`, `app/views/`, `app/jobs/` (async jobs).
- `lib/redmine/` — cross-cutting framework: `nested_set/`, `helpers/gantt.rb`, `helpers/time_report.rb`, `notifiable.rb`, `wiki_formatting/macros.rb`, `field_format.rb`, `reaction.rb`, `safe_attributes.rb`.
- `lib/plugins/acts_as_*` — shared model mixins (attachable, customizable, watchable, searchable, event, activity_provider, tree).
- `test/unit`, `test/functional`, `test/fixtures` — Test::Unit suite with YAML fixtures.

**The central record:** `app/models/issue.rb` (`class Issue < ApplicationRecord`, `issue.rb:20`, 2151 lines). Everything in the tracker revolves around it — created/edited/destroyed via `IssuesController` (`app/controllers/issues_controller.rb`), commented on and changed through `Journal`, logged against by `TimeEntry`, linked via `IssueRelation` and `Changeset`, and reported on by `IssueQuery`/Gantt/calendar/reports.

## Step 2 — The Issue contract and its shared pieces

**Model:** `app/models/issue.rb:20`.

**Included framework concerns:**
- `Redmine::SafeAttributes` — `issue.rb:21` (mass-assignment whitelist).
- `Redmine::Utils::DateCalculation` — `issue.rb:22`.
- `Redmine::I18n` — `issue.rb:23`.
- `Redmine::NestedSet::IssueNestedSet` — `issue.rb:29` → `lib/redmine/nested_set/issue_nested_set.rb:22` (parent/child tree: `lft/rgt/root_id`, `destroy_children`, `remove_from_nested_set`).
- `Redmine::Reaction::Reactable` — `issue.rb:30` → `lib/redmine/reaction.rb:35` (adds `has_many :reactions, dependent: :delete_all`).
- `Issue::Webhookable` — `issue.rb:64` → `app/models/concerns/issue/webhookable.rb:20` (`webhook_payload`).

**Plugin-style behaviors (acts_as):**
- `acts_as_attachable :after_add/:after_remove` — `issue.rb:48` → `lib/plugins/acts_as_attachable/lib/acts_as_attachable.rb:52` (file attachments).
- `acts_as_customizable` — `issue.rb:49` → `.../acts_as_customizable.rb:28` (custom field values; overrides `available_custom_fields` at `issue.rb:289`).
- `acts_as_watchable` — `issue.rb:50` → `.../acts_as_watchable.rb:29` (watchers, `notified_watchers`).
- `acts_as_searchable` — `issue.rb:51` → `.../acts_as_searchable.rb:39` (full-text search on subject/description).
- `acts_as_event` — `issue.rb:55` → `.../acts_as_event.rb:28` (activity/search result rendering).
- `acts_as_activity_provider` — `issue.rb:59` → `.../acts_as_activity_provider.rb:28` (activity feed).
- `acts_as_mentionable :attributes => ['description']` — `issue.rb:62` → `lib/redmine/acts/mentionable.rb:28`.
- `acts_as_webhookable` — `issue.rb:63` → `lib/redmine/acts/webhookable.rb:28` (emits created/updated/deleted webhooks).

**Associations other records hang off (`issue.rb:32–46`):**
- `belongs_to`: `project`, `tracker`, `status (IssueStatus)`, `author (User)`, `assigned_to (Principal)`, `fixed_version (Version)`, `priority (IssuePriority)`, `category (IssueCategory)`.
- `has_many :journals, as: :journalized, dependent: :destroy` (`issue.rb:41`) — history/comments.
- `has_many :time_entries, dependent: :destroy` (`issue.rb:42`).
- `has_and_belongs_to_many :changesets` (`issue.rb:43`).
- `has_many :relations_from / :relations_to (IssueRelation), dependent: :delete_all` (`issue.rb:45–46`).

**What a teammate must know before changing it:** The destroy contract is split across mechanisms — `dependent: :destroy` (journals, time_entries), `dependent: :delete_all` (relations, reactions — *skip callbacks*), nested-set children cascade-destroy (`issue_nested_set.rb:134`), and HABTM changesets (join rows only). The dense callback chain (`issue.rb:117–134`) ordering is load-bearing and documented inline (e.g. `add_auto_watcher` must register after `send_notification` because after-callbacks run in reverse). `delegate :notes` to `current_journal` (`issue.rb:70`) couples editing to the journal. `visible_condition` (`issue.rb:137`) is interpolated as raw SQL into many other queries.

## Step 3 — Lifecycle on create/update/destroy

**Trigger points:** `IssuesController#create` (`issues_controller.rb:151`), `#update` → `save_issue_with_child_records` (`issues_controller.rb:198, 668`), `#bulk_update` (`issues_controller.rb:349`), `#destroy` (`issues_controller.rb:431`). Also `MailHandler` (`app/models/mail_handler.rb:201, 232`) and `Changeset.scan_comment_for_issue_ids` (`changeset.rb:124`).

**Before-save chain (inline, same transaction):** `default_assign`, `clear_disabled_fields` (`issue.rb:25–27`); `set_parent_id` (`issue.rb:28`); `close_duplicates`, `update_done_ratio_from_issue_status`, `force_updated_on_change`, `update_closed_on` (`issue.rb:117–118`).

**After-save chain (inline):**
- `reschedule_following_issues` (`issue.rb:125` → `:2000`) — cascades date shifts to `precedes`-related issues (rollup).
- `update_nested_set_attributes` (`issue.rb:125` → `:1836`) — parent/child tree maintenance.
- `update_parent_attributes` (`issue.rb:125,124` → `:1856`) — parent done-ratio / dates / estimated-hours rollup; also fired `after_destroy` (`issue.rb:124`).
- `delete_selected_attachments`, `create_journal` (`issue.rb:126` → `:2049`) — persists the in-memory `current_journal` (history + journalized attribute diff via `Journal#journalize_changes`, `journal.rb:266`).
- `after_project_change` (`issue.rb:119–122` → `:1734`).

**After-commit (post-transaction):**
- `send_notification` (`issue.rb:132` → `:2099`) → `Mailer.deliver_issue_add` (`mailer.rb:105`) → `issue_add(...).deliver_later` (`mailer.rb:108`) — **async** (ActiveJob).
- `add_auto_watcher` (`issue.rb:133` → `:2090`) — inline at commit.
- `create_parent_issue_journal` (`issue.rb:134` → `:2055`) — writes a `child_id` journal entry on the old/new parent (inline, locked transaction).
- Update notifications: `Journal#send_notification` (`journal.rb:66`) → `Mailer.deliver_issue_edit` (`mailer.rb:141`) → `.deliver_later` (`mailer.rb:147`) — **async**.

**Destroy:** `Issue#destroy` (`issue.rb:260`, stale-object retry) → `dependent: :destroy` journals & time_entries; `delete_all` relations & reactions; nested-set `destroy_children` (`issue_nested_set.rb:134`); `after_destroy :update_parent_attributes`. Controller pre-step reassigns/nullifies time entries by `params[:todo]` *before* destroy (`issues_controller.rb:436–468`). Webhook `deleted` event fires via `acts_as_webhookable`.

## Step 5 — How issue identity and visibility are guarded

**Core SQL guard:** `Issue.visible_condition(user, options)` (`issue.rb:137`) → delegates to `Project.allowed_to_condition(user, :view_issues)`, then layers `issues_visibility` role rule (`all` / `default` = not-private-or-author-or-assignee / `own`) and per-tracker `view_issues` permission (`issue.rb:159–166`). The `:visible` scope wraps it (`issue.rb:84–87`).

**Per-record guard:** `Issue#visible?(usr)` (`issue.rb:172`) — same logic in Ruby for a loaded record; used before showing/acting on a single issue.

**Where guards are applied across subsystems:**
- Reports/queries: `IssueQuery` interpolates `Issue.visible_condition` into the subtask estimated-hours subquery (`issue_query.rb:62`); base list goes through the `visible` scope.
- Time report / timelog: `TimeEntry.visible` join adds `AND (#{Issue.visible_condition(User.current)})` (`time_entry.rb:68–70`); `time_entry_query.rb` resolves issue subtree ids.
- Gantt: `@query.issues(...)` (visible scope) + project visibility `Project.visible` (`gantt.rb:160,188`).
- Email/journals: `Journal.visible` scope filters `Issue.visible_condition` (`journal.rb:73`); `Journal#notified_users` re-checks `view_private_notes` per recipient (`journal.rb:187–189`); journal detail visibility re-checks `Issue.find_by_id(...).visible?(user)` (`journal.rb:127`).
- Autocomplete: `Issue.cross_project_scope(@project, params[:scope]).visible` (`auto_completes_controller.rb:29`).
- Wiki macro: `Issue.visible.find_by(id:)` (`macros.rb:368`).
- Relations: `IssueRelation` resolves the target via `Issue.visible(user).find_by_id` (`issue_relation.rb:96`).
- Parent-journal write: `Issue.visible.lock.find_by_id(old/new_parent_id)` (`issue.rb:2071,2081`).
- Controller authorization: `before_action :authorize` in `IssuesController`; `find_issue`/`find_issues` in `application_controller.rb` raise `Forbidden`/`Unauthorized` if not visible; `destroy` re-checks `@issues.all?(&:deletable?)` (`issues_controller.rb:432`) → `deletable?` (`issue.rb:228`).

**Identity/staleness guards:** `Issue#destroy` rescues `StaleObjectError`/`RecordNotFound` and retries or marks destroyed (`issue.rb:260–273`); destroy loop reloads each issue and swallows `RecordNotFound` for already-cascaded children (`issues_controller.rb:468–471`); `create_parent_issue_journal` takes a row lock (`issue.rb:2070`).

## Step 6 — Blast radius of the contract change (storage + teardown of attached data)

Ranked by risk, given the change targets how Issue stores and **tears down** attached data:

**Highest risk (teardown semantics / data loss):**
1. `Issue` association `dependent` declarations — `issue.rb:41–46` (journals/time_entries `:destroy`; relations `:delete_all`) + reactions `delete_all` (`reaction.rb:39`) + nested-set child cascade (`issue_nested_set.rb:134`). Changing any cascade can orphan or wrongly delete rows. **Re-verify** `issue_test.rb`, `issue_subtasking_test.rb`, `issue_nested_set_test.rb`.
2. `IssuesController#destroy` time-entry `todo` reassign/nullify (`issues_controller.rb:436–468`) — runs *outside* model callbacks; if storage changes, this hand-rolled path can desync. **Re-verify** `issues_controller_test.rb`, `timelog_controller_test.rb`.
3. `TimeEntry#belongs_to :issue` + validation/visibility join (`time_entry.rb:25,68,185`) — orphaned/nullified time entries break time reports.
4. `Journal` history engine (`journal.rb:24,27,231,266`) — diff/restore relies on `journalized` + `journalize_changes`; storage changes to attributes break history. **Re-verify** `journal_test.rb`, `journal_observer_test.rb`.
5. `IssueRelation` `delete_all` + `reschedule_following_issues` rollup (`issue.rb:45,2000`) — relation teardown and date cascades.
6. Nested-set parent rollups: `update_parent_attributes` / `update_nested_set_attributes` (`issue.rb:1836,1856`) fired on save *and destroy* — rollup correctness after child teardown.

**Medium risk (derive/aggregate from issue storage):**
7. `Project#has_many :issues dependent: :destroy` + `issue_changes through: :issues` (`project.rb:40–41`) and `DestroyProjectJob` — project teardown re-enters issue teardown in bulk/async.
8. `Version` fixed-issues nullify + spent-hours (`version.rb:127,256`); `IssueCategory` nullify (`issue_category.rb:24`); `User`/`Group` reassign-on-delete (`user.rb:996`, `group.rb:119`).
9. `IssueQuery` / `TimeEntryQuery` raw-SQL column/filter interpolation of `Issue.table_name`/`visible_condition` (`issue_query.rb:24–69`, `time_entry_query.rb:196`) — column renames break silently.
10. `Changeset` HABTM + commit-scan auto-update (`changeset.rb:24,253`) — relinking on SCM sync.
11. Gantt/time_report rendering (`gantt.rb:160`, `time_report.rb:110`).

**Lower risk (read/format/notify):**
12. Webhooks `created/updated/deleted` payload (`concerns/issue/webhookable.rb`, `webhook_job.rb`) — deleted-event payload may reference torn-down data.
13. Mailer add/edit/reminder + `notified_users` (`mailer.rb:105,141,615`; `issue.rb:1148`).
14. Helpers/views/macros (`issues_helper.rb`, `application_helper.rb:link_to_issue`, `macros.rb:368`, `app/views/issues/*`).
15. Search/activity/event providers (`acts_as_searchable/event/activity_provider`).

I now have a complete picture of the Issue contract and its dependents. Here is the full audit.

---

# Issue Contract Audit — Redmine

## Step 1 — Orientation

**Project:** Redmine, a Ruby on Rails 8.1 project-management / issue-tracking application (`Gemfile:5`). Standard Rails MVC layout under `app/` with substantial domain logic in `lib/redmine/` and a set of in-tree "acts_as" plugins under `lib/plugins/`.

**Layout:**
- `app/models/` — ActiveRecord domain (62 models). `app/controllers/` (~58), `app/helpers/`, `app/views/`, `app/jobs/` (async jobs).
- `lib/redmine/` — cross-cutting framework: `nested_set/`, `helpers/gantt.rb`, `helpers/time_report.rb`, `notifiable.rb`, `wiki_formatting/macros.rb`, `field_format.rb`, `reaction.rb`, `safe_attributes.rb`.
- `lib/plugins/acts_as_*` — shared model mixins (attachable, customizable, watchable, searchable, event, activity_provider, tree).
- `test/unit`, `test/functional`, `test/fixtures` — Test::Unit suite with YAML fixtures.

**The central record:** `app/models/issue.rb` (`class Issue < ApplicationRecord`, `issue.rb:20`, 2151 lines). Everything in the tracker revolves around it — created/edited/destroyed via `IssuesController` (`app/controllers/issues_controller.rb`), commented on and changed through `Journal`, logged against by `TimeEntry`, linked via `IssueRelation` and `Changeset`, and reported on by `IssueQuery`/Gantt/calendar/reports.

## Step 2 — The Issue contract and its shared pieces

**Model:** `app/models/issue.rb:20`.

**Included framework concerns:**
- `Redmine::SafeAttributes` — `issue.rb:21` (mass-assignment whitelist).
- `Redmine::Utils::DateCalculation` — `issue.rb:22`.
- `Redmine::I18n` — `issue.rb:23`.
- `Redmine::NestedSet::IssueNestedSet` — `issue.rb:29` → `lib/redmine/nested_set/issue_nested_set.rb:22` (parent/child tree: `lft/rgt/root_id`, `destroy_children`, `remove_from_nested_set`).
- `Redmine::Reaction::Reactable` — `issue.rb:30` → `lib/redmine/reaction.rb:35` (adds `has_many :reactions, dependent: :delete_all`).
- `Issue::Webhookable` — `issue.rb:64` → `app/models/concerns/issue/webhookable.rb:20` (`webhook_payload`).

**Plugin-style behaviors (acts_as):**
- `acts_as_attachable :after_add/:after_remove` — `issue.rb:48` → `lib/plugins/acts_as_attachable/lib/acts_as_attachable.rb:52` (file attachments).
- `acts_as_customizable` — `issue.rb:49` → `.../acts_as_customizable.rb:28` (custom field values; overrides `available_custom_fields` at `issue.rb:289`).
- `acts_as_watchable` — `issue.rb:50` → `.../acts_as_watchable.rb:29` (watchers, `notified_watchers`).
- `acts_as_searchable` — `issue.rb:51` → `.../acts_as_searchable.rb:39` (full-text search on subject/description).
- `acts_as_event` — `issue.rb:55` → `.../acts_as_event.rb:28` (activity/search result rendering).
- `acts_as_activity_provider` — `issue.rb:59` → `.../acts_as_activity_provider.rb:28` (activity feed).
- `acts_as_mentionable :attributes => ['description']` — `issue.rb:62` → `lib/redmine/acts/mentionable.rb:28`.
- `acts_as_webhookable` — `issue.rb:63` → `lib/redmine/acts/webhookable.rb:28` (emits created/updated/deleted webhooks).

**Associations other records hang off (`issue.rb:32–46`):**
- `belongs_to`: `project`, `tracker`, `status (IssueStatus)`, `author (User)`, `assigned_to (Principal)`, `fixed_version (Version)`, `priority (IssuePriority)`, `category (IssueCategory)`.
- `has_many :journals, as: :journalized, dependent: :destroy` (`issue.rb:41`) — history/comments.
- `has_many :time_entries, dependent: :destroy` (`issue.rb:42`).
- `has_and_belongs_to_many :changesets` (`issue.rb:43`).
- `has_many :relations_from / :relations_to (IssueRelation), dependent: :delete_all` (`issue.rb:45–46`).

**What a teammate must know before changing it:** The destroy contract is split across mechanisms — `dependent: :destroy` (journals, time_entries), `dependent: :delete_all` (relations, reactions — *skip callbacks*), nested-set children cascade-destroy (`issue_nested_set.rb:134`), and HABTM changesets (join rows only). The dense callback chain (`issue.rb:117–134`) ordering is load-bearing and documented inline (e.g. `add_auto_watcher` must register after `send_notification` because after-callbacks run in reverse). `delegate :notes` to `current_journal` (`issue.rb:70`) couples editing to the journal. `visible_condition` (`issue.rb:137`) is interpolated as raw SQL into many other queries.

## Step 3 — Lifecycle on create/update/destroy

**Trigger points:** `IssuesController#create` (`issues_controller.rb:151`), `#update` → `save_issue_with_child_records` (`issues_controller.rb:198, 668`), `#bulk_update` (`issues_controller.rb:349`), `#destroy` (`issues_controller.rb:431`). Also `MailHandler` (`app/models/mail_handler.rb:201, 232`) and `Changeset.scan_comment_for_issue_ids` (`changeset.rb:124`).

**Before-save chain (inline, same transaction):** `default_assign`, `clear_disabled_fields` (`issue.rb:25–27`); `set_parent_id` (`issue.rb:28`); `close_duplicates`, `update_done_ratio_from_issue_status`, `force_updated_on_change`, `update_closed_on` (`issue.rb:117–118`).

**After-save chain (inline):**
- `reschedule_following_issues` (`issue.rb:125` → `:2000`) — cascades date shifts to `precedes`-related issues (rollup).
- `update_nested_set_attributes` (`issue.rb:125` → `:1836`) — parent/child tree maintenance.
- `update_parent_attributes` (`issue.rb:125,124` → `:1856`) — parent done-ratio / dates / estimated-hours rollup; also fired `after_destroy` (`issue.rb:124`).
- `delete_selected_attachments`, `create_journal` (`issue.rb:126` → `:2049`) — persists the in-memory `current_journal` (history + journalized attribute diff via `Journal#journalize_changes`, `journal.rb:266`).
- `after_project_change` (`issue.rb:119–122` → `:1734`).

**After-commit (post-transaction):**
- `send_notification` (`issue.rb:132` → `:2099`) → `Mailer.deliver_issue_add` (`mailer.rb:105`) → `issue_add(...).deliver_later` (`mailer.rb:108`) — **async** (ActiveJob).
- `add_auto_watcher` (`issue.rb:133` → `:2090`) — inline at commit.
- `create_parent_issue_journal` (`issue.rb:134` → `:2055`) — writes a `child_id` journal entry on the old/new parent (inline, locked transaction).
- Update notifications: `Journal#send_notification` (`journal.rb:66`) → `Mailer.deliver_issue_edit` (`mailer.rb:141`) → `.deliver_later` (`mailer.rb:147`) — **async**.

**Destroy:** `Issue#destroy` (`issue.rb:260`, stale-object retry) → `dependent: :destroy` journals & time_entries; `delete_all` relations & reactions; nested-set `destroy_children` (`issue_nested_set.rb:134`); `after_destroy :update_parent_attributes`. Controller pre-step reassigns/nullifies time entries by `params[:todo]` *before* destroy (`issues_controller.rb:436–468`). Webhook `deleted` event fires via `acts_as_webhookable`.

## Step 4 — Every dependent of the Issue contract

### A. Request handling (controllers)
- `app/controllers/issues_controller.rb:151,198,431` — primary CRUD + bulk + destroy/reassign.
- `app/controllers/application_controller.rb` (`find_issue`/`find_issues` helpers) — shared issue loaders for all issue routes.
- `app/controllers/context_menus_controller.rb` — bulk context menu over selected issues.

### B. Models and associations
- `app/models/journal.rb:24,27` — `belongs_to :journalized` (polymorphic) + `belongs_to :issue` alias; history engine, `journalize_changes`/`journalize_relation`/`journalize_attachment`.

## Step 5 — How issue identity and visibility are guarded

**Core SQL guard:** `Issue.visible_condition(user, options)` (`issue.rb:137`) → delegates to `Project.allowed_to_condition(user, :view_issues)`, then layers `issues_visibility` role rule (`all` / `default` = not-private-or-author-or-assignee / `own`) and per-tracker `view_issues` permission (`issue.rb:159–166`). The `:visible` scope wraps it (`issue.rb:84–87`).

**Per-record guard:** `Issue#visible?(usr)` (`issue.rb:172`) — same logic in Ruby for a loaded record; used before showing/acting on a single issue.

**Where guards are applied across subsystems:**
- Reports/queries: `IssueQuery` interpolates `Issue.visible_condition` into the subtask estimated-hours subquery (`issue_query.rb:62`); base list goes through the `visible` scope.
- Time report / timelog: `TimeEntry.visible` join adds `AND (#{Issue.visible_condition(User.current)})` (`time_entry.rb:68–70`); `time_entry_query.rb` resolves issue subtree ids.
- Gantt: `@query.issues(...)` (visible scope) + project visibility `Project.visible` (`gantt.rb:160,188`).
- Email/journals: `Journal.visible` scope filters `Issue.visible_condition` (`journal.rb:73`); `Journal#notified_users` re-checks `view_private_notes` per recipient (`journal.rb:187–189`); journal detail visibility re-checks `Issue.find_by_id(...).visible?(user)` (`journal.rb:127`).
- Autocomplete: `Issue.cross_project_scope(@project, params[:scope]).visible` (`auto_completes_controller.rb:29`).
- Wiki macro: `Issue.visible.find_by(id:)` (`macros.rb:368`).
- Relations: `IssueRelation` resolves the target via `Issue.visible(user).find_by_id` (`issue_relation.rb:96`).
- Parent-journal write: `Issue.visible.lock.find_by_id(old/new_parent_id)` (`issue.rb:2071,2081`).
- Controller authorization: `before_action :authorize` in `IssuesController`; `find_issue`/`find_issues` in `application_controller.rb` raise `Forbidden`/`Unauthorized` if not visible; `destroy` re-checks `@issues.all?(&:deletable?)` (`issues_controller.rb:432`) → `deletable?` (`issue.rb:228`).

**Identity/staleness guards:** `Issue#destroy` rescues `StaleObjectError`/`RecordNotFound` and retries or marks destroyed (`issue.rb:260–273`); destroy loop reloads each issue and swallows `RecordNotFound` for already-cascaded children (`issues_controller.rb:468–471`); `create_parent_issue_journal` takes a row lock (`issue.rb:2070`).

## Step 6 — Blast radius of the contract change (storage + teardown of attached data)

Ranked by risk, given the change targets how Issue stores and **tears down** attached data:

**Highest risk (teardown semantics / data loss):**
1. `Issue` association `dependent` declarations — `issue.rb:41–46` (journals/time_entries `:destroy`; relations `:delete_all`) + reactions `delete_all` (`reaction.rb:39`) + nested-set child cascade (`issue_nested_set.rb:134`). Changing any cascade can orphan or wrongly delete rows. **Re-verify** `issue_test.rb`, `issue_subtasking_test.rb`, `issue_nested_set_test.rb`.
2. `IssuesController#destroy` time-entry `todo` reassign/nullify (`issues_controller.rb:436–468`) — runs *outside* model callbacks; if storage changes, this hand-rolled path can desync. **Re-verify** `issues_controller_test.rb`, `timelog_controller_test.rb`.
3. `TimeEntry#belongs_to :issue` + validation/visibility join (`time_entry.rb:25,68,185`) — orphaned/nullified time entries break time reports.
4. `Journal` history engine (`journal.rb:24,27,231,266`) — diff/restore relies on `journalized` + `journalize_changes`; storage changes to attributes break history. **Re-verify** `journal_test.rb`, `journal_observer_test.rb`.
5. `IssueRelation` `delete_all` + `reschedule_following_issues` rollup (`issue.rb:45,2000`) — relation teardown and date cascades.
6. Nested-set parent rollups: `update_parent_attributes` / `update_nested_set_attributes` (`issue.rb:1836,1856`) fired on save *and destroy* — rollup correctness after child teardown.

**Medium risk (derive/aggregate from issue storage):**
7. `Project#has_many :issues dependent: :destroy` + `issue_changes through: :issues` (`project.rb:40–41`) and `DestroyProjectJob` — project teardown re-enters issue teardown in bulk/async.
8. `Version` fixed-issues nullify + spent-hours (`version.rb:127,256`); `IssueCategory` nullify (`issue_category.rb:24`); `User`/`Group` reassign-on-delete (`user.rb:996`, `group.rb:119`).
9. `IssueQuery` / `TimeEntryQuery` raw-SQL column/filter interpolation of `Issue.table_name`/`visible_condition` (`issue_query.rb:24–69`, `time_entry_query.rb:196`) — column renames break silently.
10. `Changeset` HABTM + commit-scan auto-update (`changeset.rb:24,253`) — relinking on SCM sync.
11. Gantt/time_report rendering (`gantt.rb:160`, `time_report.rb:110`).

**Lower risk (read/format/notify):**
12. Webhooks `created/updated/deleted` payload (`concerns/issue/webhookable.rb`, `webhook_job.rb`) — deleted-event payload may reference torn-down data.
13. Mailer add/edit/reminder + `notified_users` (`mailer.rb:105,141,615`; `issue.rb:1148`).
14. Helpers/views/macros (`issues_helper.rb`, `application_helper.rb:link_to_issue`, `macros.rb:368`, `app/views/issues/*`).
15. Search/activity/event providers (`acts_as_searchable/event/activity_provider`).
